# AI Coding Instructions for Playwright UI Automation

## Project Overview
This is a **Playwright-based UI automation testing framework** for e-commerce and web applications. It uses the **Page Object Model (POM)** pattern to organize test code, with test data externalized to JSON files. Tests span UI-based flows and API testing.

## Architecture & Key Components

### Page Object Model Pattern
- **Location**: [pageobjects/](pageobjects/)
- **Structure**: Each application page/feature gets its own class (e.g., `LoginPage.js`, `DashboardPage.js`)
- **Pattern**: Pages expose high-level methods (e.g., `validLogin()`, `searchProductAddCart()`) that hide locator details
- **Manager**: [POManager.js](pageobjects/POManager.js) centralizes page object instantiation and acts as a facade

**Example**: When writing tests, use the manager pattern:
```javascript
const poManager = new POManager(page);
const loginPage = poManager.getLoginPage();
await loginPage.validLogin(email, password);
```

### Test Organization
- **tests/**: Mixed test files covering different scenarios (unit/integration/E2E)
- **e2e/**: Dedicated end-to-end test flows
- **test-utils/**: External test data in JSON format (credentials, product names, test parameters)

### Playwright Configuration
- **Config file**: [playwright.config.js](playwright.config.js)
- **Defaults**: Headless mode, chromium browser, 20s test timeout, 5s expect timeout
- **Reporting**: HTML reporter generates reports in `playwright-report/`
- **Screenshots/Traces**: Captured on failure only; traces always enabled
- **CI Behavior**: 2 retries on CI, single worker; local: parallel execution

## Development Workflows

### Running Tests
```bash
npm run regression          # All tests
npm run webTests           # @Web tagged tests only
npm run APITests           # @API tagged tests only
```

### Key Patterns
- **Test Tagging**: Use `@Web` and `@API` tags to organize test suites
- **Test Data**: JSON files in `test-utils/` are imported and iterated (see [eComm_e2e_POM.spec.js](tests/eComm_e2e_POM.spec.js))
- **Async/Await**: All Playwright actions are async; use proper await chains
- **Waits**: Use `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` for page loads, not arbitrary sleeps

### API Testing Pattern
Tests marked with `@API` tag test backend services. See [APIpractice.spec.js](tests/APIpractice.spec.js) for request/response patterns.

## Common Conventions

### Naming
- **Test files**: Descriptive names with `.spec.js` suffix
- **POM methods**: Verb-first (e.g., `validLogin()`, `searchProductAddCart()`, `navigatetoCart()`)
- **Test variables**: camelCase for data, descriptive names for UI elements

### Locators
- **Preferred**: CSS selectors and data attributes
- **Fallback**: XPath for complex DOM queries
- **Pattern**: Store all locators in constructor, expose via public methods

### Error Handling
- No try-catch blocks in tests; let Playwright handle assertion failures
- Use `expect()` for test assertions only
- Conditional logic OK for product filtering (see [end2endPractice.spec.js](tests/end2endPractice.spec.js#L35) for loop pattern)

### Cleanup
- Use `browser.newContext()` to isolate tests
- Always `context.close()` after test completion
- No manual storage/cookie cleanup needed unless required by app state

## Integration Points

### External Applications (Test Targets)
- **Primary**: `https://rahulshettyacademy.com/client/` - E-commerce app
- **Secondary**: `https://www.saucedemo.com/` - Practice e-commerce
- **Credentials**: Externalized in [test-utils/eComm_e2e_POMtestData.json](test-utils/eComm_e2e_POMtestData.json)

### Dependencies
- `@playwright/test`: Test runner and assertions
- `allure-playwright`: Test report integration (results in `allure-results/`)
- `@types/node`: TypeScript type support

### Report Generation
- **HTML Reports**: Auto-generated by Playwright in `playwright-report/`
- **Allure Reports**: Run `allure serve allure-results/` after tests (if Allure CLI installed)

## Code Quality Standards

### When Adding New Tests
1. Create corresponding Page Object class in `pageobjects/` if testing new page
2. Add test data to JSON in `test-utils/` if using parametrized data
3. Use POManager to access pages; avoid direct page instantiation
4. Tag tests with `@Web` or `@API` for filtering
5. Use high-level POM methods; avoid inline locators

### When Adding POM Classes
1. Constructor stores all locators as `this.locatorName = page.locator(...)`
2. Export class with `module.exports = {ClassName}`
3. Register in [POManager.js](pageobjects/POManager.js) with getter method
4. Methods should handle waits internally (don't expose wait logic)

### When Modifying Tests
- Preserve test data externalization; update JSON files, not hardcoded values
- Keep test steps at high business level (login → search → add to cart → checkout)
- If adding complex DOM traversal, extract to POM method

## Common Pitfalls to Avoid
- **Don't**: Use `page.goto()` directly in tests; encapsulate in POM (see [LoginPage.js](pageobjects/LoginPage.js#L8))
- **Don't**: Hardcode credentials; always use test data JSON
- **Don't**: Mix UI and API logic in same test; separate by `@Web`/`@API` tags
- **Don't**: Add sleeps; use Playwright's built-in waits (`waitFor()`, `waitForLoadState()`)
- **Don't**: Create multiple browser contexts in same test unless intentional (test isolation)
